assumptions: mobile-first remains primary unless client analytics contradict it

product.subject: fitness coaching and class booking
product.primaryJob: discover, compare, and book the right fitness service

direction.thesis: A dark training interface that feels like calibrated sports equipment rather than a generic gaming dashboard.
direction.metaphor: measured physical cadence and training instruments
direction.signatureElement: A progress rail that behaves like an interval-training timeline and connects discovery, booking, and attendance.
forbiddenPatterns: all content inside identical rounded cards

visualSystem.rationale: The interface uses Material 3 adaptive structure and state behavior, then adds a training-instrument visual layer so booking feels precise, energetic, and task-first for athletes and new gym members.

themeFingerprint.paletteModel: dark neutral equipment surfaces with controlled signal-red action roles
themeFingerprint.typographyModel: condensed display for momentum cues, readable bilingual UI text, tabular numeric session data
themeFingerprint.geometryModel: subtle container radius with sharper control rhythm for a calibrated equipment feel
themeFingerprint.spacingModel: mobile-first touch spacing with compact comparison rows
themeFingerprint.surfaceModel: tonal Material 3 surfaces with product-specific rail and comparison panels
themeFingerprint.borderModel: low-chroma outlines for separation on dark surfaces
themeFingerprint.elevationModel: mostly flat tonal surfaces with elevation reserved for sheets and dialogs
themeFingerprint.layoutModel: booking-first mobile flow that expands into split discovery/detail views
themeFingerprint.navigationModel: bottom navigation on mobile, expanded rail on tablet and desktop
themeFingerprint.imageryModel: real class and coach imagery with performance-data overlays
themeFingerprint.iconographyModel: outlined functional icons with sport-status accents
themeFingerprint.motionModel: functional transitions plus one interval-progress confirmation sequence
themeFingerprint.densityModel: balanced mobile density, denser desktop comparison
themeFingerprint.signatureElement: interval progress rail connecting discovery, booking, and attendance

distinctivenessChecks.notes: The interval rail, athletic typography, booking-first navigation, and calibrated surface model would not fit a generic B2B dashboard.

dials.notes: Distinctive composition and one strong motion sequence; normal task interactions remain restrained.

input.value: dark red and black sporty theme

typography.display: A condensed or athletic display face with full required language support
typography.body: A highly readable UI family with Persian and Latin compatibility
typography.utility: A tabular-number face for time, distance, price, and progress data

layout.mobile: task-first bottom navigation and edge-to-edge primary flow
layout.tablet: split discovery and detail when space permits
layout.desktop: expanded application shell, not a stretched phone frame

layoutModel.contentStructure: booking-first flow with discover, compare, detail, confirm, and attendance status
layoutModel.navigationPattern: mobile bottom navigation, tablet rail, desktop expanded shell
layoutModel.gridBehavior: single-column mobile flow that becomes split discovery/detail on larger viewports
layoutModel.hierarchyStrategy: primary session action stays visually dominant while comparison data remains scannable
layoutModel.responsiveTransformation: mobile task steps expand into side-by-side browsing and booking context
layoutModel.reasoning: fitness booking is often mobile and time-sensitive, but desktop users benefit from faster comparison.

motion.mainSequence: interval-like progress movement during onboarding and booking confirmation
motion.functionalFeedback: short state transitions for filters, selections, and validation
motion.reducedMotion: instant state changes with opacity-only feedback

databaseInputs: used only to compare fitness UX patterns and accessibility risks

research.evidenceStatus: proposal and user-stated direction only; no validated end-user research

claims.claim: mobile booking should be the primary journey
new members value fast service comparison

validationNeeded: compare booking-first and progress-first home-screen models with target users

content.voice: direct, active, supportive without motivational filler
stressTests: mixed LTR prices, phone numbers, and URLs
